error_message:
EMAIL_RE in apply.ts uses [\s@] character class — accepts CR/LF in email payload (header-injection risk). Unified header-injection-safe regex /[^
@\s]+@[^
@\s]+\.[^
@\s]+$/ lives in src/lib/forms/validators.ts:emailNZ. apply.ts still imports its own copy.
root_cause: Three duplicated copies of email regex (apply.ts:35 + checkout-pm.ts:7 + checkout-am.ts:7). checkout-pm/am used CR/LF-safe class [\r\n@\s], apply.ts used weaker [\s@]. Diff not noticed when each route was refactored independently.
fix: Phase A: emailNZ exported from src/lib/forms/validators.ts with the safe class. Tests assert CR/LF payloads (`a@b.com\r\nBcc: v@y.com`) are rejected. Phase C: apply.ts validation migrates to use emailNZ, closing the bug at the call site.
